text: The initial routing model was a taxonomy without an explicit decision procedure for ambiguous cases.
`inbox/` was treated as architecture without defined naming, lifecycle, cleanup, or discoverability rules.
The draft dismissed a routing skill too quickly and failed to engage with the value of enforcement at execution time.
Munin's role in task handoff was underspecified, especially for Desktop/Web/Mobile environments without repo access.
The draft did not clearly separate 'inform' from 'command' as distinct operations.
The strongest practical recommendation was buried; the draft needed a sharper default rather than an abstract principle.
Even after revision, the handoff-file discovery mechanism needed to be stated explicitly: the invoking prompt or task description must point Claude to the file.
